name: Cherry-Almond Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 325°F. Coat a baking sheet with nonstick cooking spray.
Beat eggs, egg whites, sugar, and almond extract in an electric mixer until smooth. Add flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt; beat until well mixed. Stir in cherries and almonds. If the dough is too sticky, add a few tablespoons of flour. Drop dough by large spoonfuls onto the prepared sheet to form 2 parallel lines. The spoonfuls should be close together. With lightly floured hands, shape the lines into two parallel rolls extending along each side of the sheet, leaving enough space for spreading. Bake until lightly browned and slightly firm to touch, about 20-25 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 300°F. Cool rolls on sheets for 10 minutes. Cut each roll into 20 slices using a serrated knife. Turn each slice on its side as it is cut. Return the sliced biscuits to the sheet and bake an additional 10 minutes. Flip the slices so they bake their other sides, then return them to the oven for 10-20 more minutes. The ends will be done in about 20 minutes total; the rest may need up to 30 minutes. Cool on wire racks.
